channel.title: Plaintext Security Blog
channel.description: Practical security explainers and commentary from Plaintext Security.

item.title: Why QR Codes Are a Trust Problem, Not a Tech Problem
item.description: The risk in QR codes is not the square itself; it is the invisible destination and rushed context.

item.title: One Red Flag Can Save You
item.description: You do not need perfect detection; one credible warning sign is enough reason to stop and verify.

item.title: Why Smart People Still Get Scammed
item.description: Scams succeed through context and pressure, not lack of intelligence.

item.title: Move Fast, Ship Risk: A Builder’s Tradeoff
item.description: Shipping quickly is not the problem; shipping without secure defaults is.

item.title: AI Makes Scams Cheaper, Faster, and More Personal
item.description: Generative tooling lowers attacker cost and increases iteration speed, changing scam economics.

item.title: Good Security Advice Should Be Clear Under Stress
item.description: If guidance only works when people are calm and technical, it is not good enough.
